2603.18474.title: WASD: Locating Critical Neurons as Sufficient Conditions for Explaining and Controlling LLM Behavior
2603.18474.paper_summary: Precise behavioral control of large language models (LLMs) is critical for complex applications. However, existing methods often incur high training costs, lack natural language controllability, or compromise semantic coherence. To bridge this gap, we propose WASD (unWeaving Actionable Sufficient Directives), a novel framework that explains model behavior by identifying sufficient neural conditions for token generation. Our method represents candidate conditions as neuron-activation predicates and iteratively searches for a minimal set that guarantees the current output under input perturbations. Experiments on SST-2 and CounterFact with the Gemma-2-2B model demonstrate that our approach produces explanations that are more stable, accurate, and concise than conventional attribution graphs. Moreover, through a case study on controlling cross-lingual output generation, we validated the practical effectiveness of WASD in controlling model behavior.
2603.18474.paper_summary_zh: 精確控制大型語言模型（LLMs）的行為對於複雜應用至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有的方法往往需要高昂的訓練成本，缺乏自然語言的可控性，或妥協於語義的一致性。<lb/>為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了WASD（unWeaving Actionable Sufficient Directives），這是一個新穎的框架，通過識別生成標記的充分神經條件來解釋模型行為。<lb/>我們的方法將候選條件表示為神經元激活謂詞，並迭代搜索一組最小條件，以保證在輸入擾動下當前輸出。<lb/>在SST-2和CounterFact上使用Gemma-2-2B模型的實驗表明，我們的方法產生的解釋比傳統的歸因圖更穩定、準確且簡潔。<lb/>此外，通過對跨語言輸出生成控制的案例研究，我們驗證了WASD在控制模型行為方面的實際有效性。
2603.18474.author: Haonan Yu et.al.
2603.18474.authors: Haonan Yu, Junhao Liu, Zhenyu Yan, Haoran Lin, Xin Zhang
